title: Closing Richmond's Data Gaps: Tech Paths to Youth Jobs & Maternal Health Wins
summary: Executive summary of Richmond's Thriving Families problem space: fragmented youth employment ecosystem and siloed maternal health data. Covers Youth Works RVA, DOLI work permits, GRTC Zero Fare transit, and VDH/KIDS COUNT data gaps.

title: Thriving Families, Faster: Closing Richmond's First-Job and Maternal Data Gaps in 48 Hours
summary: Comprehensive research framework spanning both Thriving Families problem statements: youth employment pathways and maternal health data coordination. Covers the full arc from problem landscape to demo strategy.

title: Thriving Families RVA MVP: The 10 Unknowns That Will Make or Break It
summary: Ten prioritized research questions whose answers determine hackathon MVP viability for both problem domains. Covers data availability, user needs, compliance risks, and demo requirements.

title: Building MVPs That Matter: Testable Paths to Thriving Families in Richmond
summary: Ten testable hypotheses for youth employment and maternal health MVPs, with validation approaches and risk flags for each. Grounds team decisions in evidence rather than assumptions.

title: Build Safely: Red Flags That Can Derail Richmond's Thriving Families Tech
summary: Warning signs and anti-patterns for both problem domains: minor data privacy risks, medical claim liability, over-reliance on unavailable data, and integration dependencies that block demos.

title: Unblocking Richmond's Thriving Families MVP: High-impact data gaps, 48-hour bridges, and risk-proof choices
summary: Catalog of critical information gaps that affect both problem statements, with practical bridging strategies for each gap that can be executed within a 48-hour hackathon window.

title: Breaking the First-Job Bottleneck: How Richmond Teens Actually Reach Work (or Don't)
summary: Root cause analysis of youth employment barriers in Richmond: fragmented program discovery, DOLI work permit sequencing, document barriers, GRTC transit dependency, and lack of a unified job marketplace for teens.

title: Richmond's Maternal Data Maze: Who Holds What, Why It Conflicts, and How to Fix It
summary: Analysis of maternal health data fragmentation in Richmond: multiple agencies holding overlapping datasets (VDH, ACS, KIDS COUNT), duplicate analysis burden on nonprofits, and the coordination gap that a shared schema could close.

title: Two Paths to Thriving Families—Which Ships by Sunday, Which Scales by Policy
summary: Side-by-side comparison of Youth Employment (D3=1, data scarce) versus Maternal Health Data Coordination (D3=3, data exists) for hackathon viability. Recommends maternal health for data-dependent teams and youth employment for content curation teams.

title: Breaking the Richmond Teen Job Funnel: Where Motivated Youth Stall—and How to Unblock Each Step
summary: Step-by-step analysis of the Richmond teen job funnel from discovery through onboarding, identifying the stall points at each stage: awareness, eligibility, documentation, compliance, and placement.

title: Richmond's Maternal Health Equity Gaps: Where to Aim the Next Dollar
summary: Equity-focused landscape of maternal health disparities in Richmond: racial and geographic disparities in outcomes, which data sources capture them, and where software tools can support targeted resource allocation.

title: Designing for Real Teens: Richmond's 14–18 Job Seekers, Their Barriers, and the Supports That Actually Move Them to Work
summary: User persona and journey for Richmond youth job seekers ages 14–18: digital access patterns, documentation barriers, trust factors, communication preferences, and what support structures actually result in job placements.

title: Richmond SMB Youth Hiring Personas: Compliance-Smart, Confidence-Ready Matches
summary: User personas for Richmond small and mid-size employers who hire youth: compliance anxiety around child labor laws, documentation requirements, and what tools would reduce friction enough to expand youth hiring.

title: Who Uses Richmond's Maternal Data—and What a Trusted Tool Must Do
summary: User persona for maternal health data users in Richmond: nonprofit researchers, grant writers, and program directors who currently duplicate data analysis. Describes what a trusted shared tool must provide to earn adoption.

title: From Classrooms to Paychecks: Mapping Richmond's Youth Employment Ecosystem and Fixing the Gaps
summary: Stakeholder map of Richmond's youth employment ecosystem: city agencies, schools, nonprofits, employers, and intermediaries. Identifies coordination gaps and which stakeholders are positioned to champion a digital solution.

title: Richmond Teens, Real Access: Designing a Mobile-First, Offline-Safe MVP
summary: Digital equity analysis for Richmond youth: smartphone vs. desktop access, data plan constraints, offline needs, and design requirements for reaching teens who lack reliable broadband. Guides MVP architecture decisions.

title: Richmond Teen Workforce Programs 2026 — Where to Place, When to Apply, What to Fix
summary: Inventory of Richmond youth workforce programs for 2026: Youth Works RVA, RPS work-based learning, Girls For A Change, Boys & Girls Club, Freedom School, CHAT. Includes eligibility windows, application deadlines, and known gaps.

title: Richmond Youth Employment: Who Serves Whom, How They Coordinate, and Where to Build Next
summary: Landscape of who serves which youth populations, how programs currently coordinate (or don't), and where technology could improve referral, tracking, or capacity matching across the ecosystem.

title: Richmond Maternal Health Data Users — Who Publishes What, Who Needs What, and Where a Shared Tool Fits Now
summary: Map of Richmond maternal health organizations and their data publishing/consuming roles: VDH, health systems, nonprofits, and community health workers. Identifies where a shared data dashboard would eliminate duplicated analysis.

title: Closing the Gaps in Richmond: Youth Jobs Access and Maternal Data Coordination
summary: Service gap analysis for both problem domains: what exists versus what is missing in Richmond's youth employment and maternal health data service landscape, with gap severity ratings.

title: Closing the Digital Gap: Youth Jobs and Maternal Health Data Tools in Richmond
summary: Assessment of existing digital tools and portals in Richmond's youth employment and maternal health spaces: what tools exist, what they fail to do, and where a hackathon prototype fills a real gap.

title: Virginia Teen Work Compliance 2026: Permits, Hours, Hazards, and Curfew—Ages 14–18
summary: Detailed reference on Virginia child labor law and DOLI work permit requirements for teens ages 14–18: permit process, hour restrictions by age, prohibited occupations, curfew rules, and I-9 documentation requirements.

title: Virginia Maternal Health Data Playbook: Pinpointing Richmond-Ready Indicators
summary: Guide to Virginia Department of Health maternal health datasets: which indicators are available at city/locality level, data update cadence, download formats, and which metrics are Richmond-ready for a hackathon dashboard.

title: ACS building blocks for a Richmond maternal health equity dashboard
summary: American Community Survey table codes and geographies for a Richmond maternal health equity dashboard: poverty rate, uninsured rate, maternal age demographics, race/ethnicity breakdowns, and how to pull them via Census API.

title: KIDS COUNT for Richmond: Maternal Health and Family Wellbeing, Ready for Action
summary: Annie E. Casey KIDS COUNT Data Center as a data source for Richmond family and maternal wellbeing indicators: available metrics, geography support, API access, and how to integrate with a hackathon dashboard.

title: GRTC GTFS, Fast: From Open Feeds to 'Nearest Bus Stop' in 48 Hours
summary: Technical guide to GRTC's GTFS transit feed for Richmond: how to download, parse, and overlay bus routes on a youth employment map. Covers Zero Fare routes, GTFS stops schema, and a 48-hour implementation path.

title: Urban Youth Employment Tech — What Works and What Richmond Needs Now
summary: Survey of youth employment technology tools in other US cities: job portals, compliance wizards, navigator apps. Identifies patterns that transferred successfully and patterns that failed, with relevance ratings for Richmond.

title: Building Trustworthy Maternal & Community Health Dashboards for Richmond Stakeholders
summary: Survey of maternal and community health dashboard implementations nationally: design patterns that build trust, data governance models, update mechanisms, and lessons learned for Richmond's context.

title: Pathways That Work: Evidence-Based Navigator Patterns for First-Time Jobseekers
summary: Evidence base for workforce navigator tools aimed at first-time youth jobseekers: what interaction patterns reduce dropout, which features produce employment outcomes, and which design choices backfire.

title: Build What Sticks: Avoiding Civic-Tech Pitfalls for Thriving Families
summary: Case studies of civic tech failures in youth employment and health data domains: over-engineered platforms, data governance breakdowns, adoption failures, and maintenance cliff edges that killed otherwise sound prototypes.

title: From Weekend Hack to Civic Impact: What Actually Works
summary: Analysis of civic hackathon projects that achieved real-world adoption: what made them succeed (narrow scope, real users, public data, handoff plan) versus projects that vanished after demo day.

title: 48-Hour Civic Tech Wins: Ranked, Low-Risk Opportunity Spaces in Youth Employment and Maternal Health
summary: Ranked list of solution opportunities across both Thriving Families domains, scored on data readiness, user clarity, demo feasibility, and risk level. Primary reference for team opportunity selection.

title: Richmond Teens to Paychecks: Fast, Software-Shaped Wins by Sunday
summary: Specific software-shaped opportunities in the youth employment domain that are buildable in 48 hours: program directory, compliance wizard, transit overlay, and employer readiness tools. Includes scope boundaries for each.

title: Richmond Maternal Data: Fast, Open, and Actionable in 48 Hours
summary: Specific software-shaped opportunities in maternal health data coordination: shared indicator dashboard, data aggregator, and reporting template generator. Uses VDH, ACS, and KIDS COUNT as open data sources.

title: Software That Actually Moves the Needle: A hard-nosed map of where code helps (and doesn't) in youth jobs and maternal health data
summary: Honest assessment of where software can and cannot make a difference in the two Thriving Families domains: distinguishing software-shaped problems from policy-shaped ones and integration-dependent ones.

title: Thriving Families Hackathon: Avoiding Regulatory Tripwires with Safer Builds
summary: Explicit list of solution directions to avoid: eligibility determination tools, medical advice features, systems requiring official integration, and anything touching minor PII. Explains the regulatory and ethical tripwires for each.

title: Build Youth Jobs Tools That Skip the Privacy Minefield
summary: Privacy risk analysis specific to youth-facing tools: COPPA implications for under-13, FERPA for school-linked data, what data can be displayed publicly versus must be anonymized, and safe design patterns.

title: Designing Truth: Preventing Harm from Maternal Health Data Misuse in Richmond's Civic-Tech Tools
summary: Risk analysis for maternal health data tools: what constitutes a harmful health claim, how to display statistics without implying medical advice, visual design guardrails, and disclaimer requirements.

title: Designing for the Teens Who Need It Most: An inclusion risk playbook for a Richmond youth jobs navigator
summary: Inclusion risk analysis for a Richmond youth jobs navigator: digital access gaps, language barriers, disability accessibility, and how design choices can inadvertently exclude the teens with the highest need.

title: Thriving Families: Data Quality Risks and No-API Mitigations
summary: Data quality risks in both problem domains: stale program listings, unreliable scraping targets, VDH data update lags, and practical mitigations for teams that cannot wait for clean data before building.

title: Building Safe-by-Design Thriving Families Tools: Guardrails That Ship
summary: Comprehensive guardrails checklist for Thriving Families tools: content limits, user flow constraints, AI behavior limits, data display rules, and review checkpoints that can be implemented within a hackathon timeline.

title: 48-Hour Civic Build Playbook: Minimum-Credible MVPs for Thriving Families (Youth Employment Edition)
summary: Framework for determining MVP credibility within 48 hours: what a minimum-credible prototype requires, how to scope features against data availability, and the demo-ready definition of done for youth employment tools.

title: 48-Hour Richmond Youth Jobs MVP — Manual Curation to Mobile Demo
summary: Concrete MVP build plan for a Richmond youth jobs navigator: manual content curation strategy, GRTC transit overlay, DOLI compliance wizard, mobile-first UI, and a step-by-step 48-hour build timeline.

title: 48-hour Richmond Maternal Health MVP — Fast, Resilient Data + Dashboard Plan
summary: Concrete MVP build plan for a Richmond maternal health data dashboard: which VDH, ACS, and KIDS COUNT datasets to use, schema normalization steps, dashboard wireframe, and a 48-hour build timeline.

title: From Zero to Useful: Shipping a Youth Jobs MVP in Richmond Without a Dataset
summary: Strategies for shipping a useful youth employment MVP when compiled datasets do not exist (D3=1): manual curation approach, seed data from public sources, and how to frame data gaps honestly in the demo.

title: Thriving Families MVP Boundaries: Build-Now Decisions That Ship
summary: Explicit scope boundary decisions for both MVP types: what must be in the demo, what can be mocked, what must be deferred, and how to defend scope decisions to judges.

title: Winning Civic-Tech Demos: 5 Proven Archetypes Judges Reward
summary: Five demo archetypes that civic tech judges consistently reward: problem-then-solution narrative, user journey walkthrough, data surfacing demo, before-and-after contrast, and the honest-about-limits close.

title: Richmond Youth Jobs Navigator — Credible, Privacy-Safe Pathways That Work
summary: Pitch guide for a Richmond youth jobs navigator demo: opening hook, data credibility evidence, user journey walkthrough, privacy and compliance framing, and the handoff statement for judges.

title: Grant-Ready Richmond Maternal Health: Credible Data, 5-Step Demo
summary: Five-step demo guide for a Richmond maternal health data dashboard: framing the data coordination problem, showing the dashboard, explaining the data sources, demonstrating equity features, and closing with a post-hackathon path.

title: Winning Civic-Tech Demos: A Credibility Playbook for Public Health and Youth-Services Judges
summary: Credibility playbook for demos presented to public health and youth-services judges: what evidence they expect, what claims to avoid, how to cite sources during a demo, and how to answer hard questions.

title: Winning with Humility: Framing Thriving Families Prototypes for Real Richmond Impact
summary: Framing strategy for presenting Thriving Families prototypes honestly: how to claim impact without overclaiming, how to present limitations as strengths, and how to connect the prototype to Richmond's actual community needs.
